title: Inventory school connectivity, mobile-network, and digital-learning data sources for low-income countries
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates Giga school-connectivity data, national school census ICT indicators, mobile-network coverage maps, and connectivity-quality studies into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit connectivity-classification methodology and data-currency assessment.
inputs: Giga school-mapping documentation and connectivity-classification methodology
National school census ICT-indicator documentation for target countries
Mobile-network coverage data from GSMA and national regulators
Published connectivity-quality studies for school internet use
UNICEF/ITU digital divide data and methodology
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly connectivity-classification methodology and data currency.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering connectivity-classification methodology, data currency, and cross-validation status.

title: Compile harmonized school-connectivity data for at least two low-income countries
outcome: A harmonized school-connectivity dataset for at least two low-income countries, documenting connectivity-classification methodology, data currency, and cross-validation between Giga and school census data where possible.
inputs: Giga school-connectivity data for target countries
National school census ICT indicators for target countries
Mobile-network coverage data for connectivity context
Published cross-validation studies between Giga and census data
expected_artifact: validation.md update with connectivity methodology and data-currency documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms connectivity data extraction, classification methodology documentation, and data-currency assessment for each target country.
done_condition: Connectivity dataset includes methodology, data-currency documentation, cross-validation assessment, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Map sub-national school connectivity gaps with quality-dimension documentation
outcome: A sub-national connectivity-gap map for at least two low-income countries, with explicit documentation of connectivity classification, data currency, and the distinction between connectivity existence and verified digital-learning use.
inputs: administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
connectivity-quality studies for bandwidth and reliability context
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with gap methodology and quality-dimension documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms gap-classification methodology, data-currency documentation, and connectivity-vs-usage distinction for each target country.
done_condition: Connectivity-gap map includes methodology, data-currency documentation, quality-dimension context, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Red-team connectivity-map overstatement and technology-procurement misuse risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how connectivity maps could overstate digital-readiness by ignoring electricity, device, and teacher-literacy gaps, how satellite classification could misclassify intermittent connectivity, and how results could drive technology procurement without complementary investments.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every connectivity claim has classification-methodology documentation, data-currency assessment, and connectivity-vs-usage disclaimers. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every connectivity claim is either blocked, softened to classification-limited language with explicit quality-dimension caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match education ministry ICT planning decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the connectivity-gap analysis would change any real decision for education ministry ICT directors or school-connectivity program managers without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for education ministry ICT audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that connectivity maps drive technology procurement without complementary investments in electricity, devices, and teacher training. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Rwanda Ministry of Education ICT director), named decision (e.g., school connectivity infrastructure targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for technology procurement without complementary investments), and replicated field-reality review.
